findings.evidence_snippet:
54: export const isAuthorized = () => expressJwt(({ secret: publicKey }) as any)
>>    55: export const denyAll = () => expressJwt({ secret: '' + Math.random() } as any)
      56: export const authorize = (user = {}) => jwt.sign(user, privateKey, { expiresIn: '6h', algorithm: 'RS256' })
findings.title: express-jwt middleware missing algorithms allowlist — lib/insecurity.ts:55
findings.scenario: The middleware is the gate for every authenticated route — a mis-configured expressJwt undermines the entire authorization surface.
findings.recommended_mitigation_title: Add `algorithms: ['RS256']` (or matching) to the expressJwt() options object.

findings.title: JWT decode used without signature verification — lib/insecurity.ts:58
findings.scenario: Decode-only flows let an attacker construct a token with any claims (admin, sub=victim, scope=*) — no key material required.
findings.recommended_mitigation_title: Replace `.decode(token)` with `.verify(token, key, { algorithms: [...] })` and gate authorization on the verified payload only.

findings.title: JWT verify call missing algorithms allowlist (algorithm confusion) — lib/insecurity.ts:191
findings.scenario: Without an explicit algorithm allowlist, attackers can forge tokens with alg:none (older lib versions) or use the public key as an HMAC secret to mint valid signatures.
findings.recommended_mitigation_title: Always pass `algorithms: ['RS256']` (or the project's chosen alg) as the third argument's options object to jwt.verify.

findings.title: Broken authorization — attacker-controlled owner ID in resource query — routes/address.ts:11
findings.scenario: Server-side authorization MUST derive the resource owner from the authenticated session (req.user / req.session / req.auth), never from attacker-controlled request data. Trusting req.body.UserId etc. enables horizontal privilege escalation across all authenticated tenants.
findings.recommended_mitigation_title: Replace req.body.UserId/userId/ownerId with req.user.id (or equivalent session-derived identity) in every WHERE/filter clause.

findings.title: Broken authorization — attacker-controlled owner ID in resource query — routes/address.ts:18
findings.scenario: Server-side authorization MUST derive the resource owner from the authenticated session (req.user / req.session / req.auth), never from attacker-controlled request data. Trusting req.body.UserId etc. enables horizontal privilege escalation across all authenticated tenants.
findings.recommended_mitigation_title: Replace req.body.UserId/userId/ownerId with req.user.id (or equivalent session-derived identity) in every WHERE/filter clause.

findings.title: Broken authorization — attacker-controlled owner ID in resource query — routes/address.ts:29
findings.scenario: Server-side authorization MUST derive the resource owner from the authenticated session (req.user / req.session / req.auth), never from attacker-controlled request data. Trusting req.body.UserId etc. enables horizontal privilege escalation across all authenticated tenants.
findings.recommended_mitigation_title: Replace req.body.UserId/userId/ownerId with req.user.id (or equivalent session-derived identity) in every WHERE/filter clause.

findings.title: IDOR — resource lookup by URL parameter without ownership filter — routes/basketItems.ts:68
findings.scenario: Any authenticated user can substitute another user's resource ID in the URL and read or modify the target record because the query filter has no ownership clause. Admin-only routes are excluded via the counter-pattern allowlist.
findings.recommended_mitigation_title: Add an owner column to the WHERE clause (UserId: req.user.id) or wrap the route in an ensureOwnership/requireRole middleware.

findings.recommended_mitigation_title: Always pass `algorithms: ['RS256']` (or the project's chosen alg) as the third argument's options object to jwt.verify.

findings.evidence_snippet:
309:   app.post('/file-upload', uploadToMemory.single('file'), ensureFileIsPassed, metrics.observeFileUploadMetricsMiddleware(), checkUploadSize, checkFileType, handleZipFileUpload, handleXmlUpload, han...
>>   310:   app.post('/profile/image/file', uploadToMemory.single('file'), ensureFileIsPassed, metrics.observeFileUploadMetricsMiddleware(), profileImageFileUpload())
     311:   app.post('/profile/image/url', uploadToMemory.single('file'), profileImageUrlUpload())
findings.title: Sensitive REST route registered without authentication middleware — server.ts:310
findings.scenario: State-changing operations on sensitive resources MUST require a proven session. A registration line that lacks any auth marker either trusts the URL itself or relies on a downstream check that the static signature cannot prove exists.
findings.recommended_mitigation_title: Add an explicit auth middleware (isAuthorized() / passport.authenticate() / requireAuth) to the route registration, or mark the route intentionally public via app.get(...) for read-only access only.
